name: Can a handyman do plumbing work, or do I need a licensed plumber?
headline: Can a handyman do plumbing work, or do I need a licensed plumber?

mainEntity.name: Can a handyman do plumbing work, or do I need a licensed plumber?
mainEntity.text: Can a handyman do plumbing work, or do I need a licensed plumber?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, most handymen can handle **small plumbing jobs**, but there are limits. In general, a handyman is fine for minor, non‑structural, non‑permit work, while a licensed plumber is needed for bigger or more complex jobs. Typical handyman‑appropriate plumbing tasks include: - Replacing faucets, showerheads, or sink drains - Fixing or replacing toilet parts (like fill valves and flappers) - Unclogging simple drains with basic tools (not major sewer line issues) - Replacing garbage disposals or dishwasher hookups, when no new lines are needed You should call a **licensed plumber** when: - You smell gas or suspect a gas line issue - Pipes need to be cut, re‑routed, or upgraded - There is a main line or sewer line backup - The work clearly needs a permit or inspection Pitfalls to avoid: - Letting anyone work on gas lines or major water lines without proper licensing. That can be dangerous and may cause insurance problems. - Ignoring leaks inside walls, ceilings, or under the house. These can turn into mold and structural damage if not handled correctly. When in doubt, describe the issue in detail and ask the handyman what they are legally allowed and experienced enough to do. A good handyman will tell you honestly when you need a plumber instead.
